reasoning: The LLM fidelity evaluation found no issues, but the programmatic evaluation identified three major structural accessibility defects related to tables. Specifically, tables are split across page breaks, a table caption is coded as a header row, and multiple tables lack proper <th> tags. Because there are between 1 and 5 major defects, this document is routed to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 14 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=14. Examples: pages 4-5: 11 cols (agent county, 2010...); pages 5-6: 11 cols (, 2010...); pages 6-7: 11 cols (, 2010...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=1. Examples: p2: "SUMMARY". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 16 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=16. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "DSS - Department of Social Services issues free su". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

action_items: Merge the 14 tables that were inappropriately split across page breaks.
Convert the single spanning header row in the summary table to a <caption> element.
Ensure all tables have properly marked <th> elements for screen reader accessibility.
Reformat the unordered list item following the table into a standard paragraph to properly represent it as a footnote.
